data.full_text:
POSITION DESCRIPTION - SUPERVISOR
Reportable to Site Senior Executive

MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Undertake quarrying and sales activities to achieve key objectives in the areas of Customer Service, Quality, Workplace Health and Safety and Environmental Management.
Conduct and record prestart meeting when required.
Direct and supervise daily roles of the workforce.
Ensure that all workers (including contractors) have the appropriate skills, expertise and competencies to undertake the tasks and activities delegated to them.
Ensure loader operators are aware of correct loading requirements and weights of trucks, to ensure that overloading does not occur.
Be aware of the Chain of Responsibility Policy.
Provide appropriate training to ensure all workers (including contractors) comply with the organisations quality, safety and environmental standards and systems.
Carry out checks to ensure contractor plant and equipment is fit for purpose as per your obligations under Contractor Management.
Ensure policies, procedures and objectives are implemented and observed within the operation.
Write purchase orders as required.
Act as spare man and operate machines when usual operator is absent.
Operate pugmill when required.
Supervise and/or conduct/assist with repairs or maintenance on crushing equipment.
Operate water truck for dust suppression when required.
Visually monitor your surrounds at all times and report any concerns immediately.
Assist Site Senior Executive with quarry development.
Assist Site Senior Executive with task preparation and planning and development of Safe Work Procedures.
Secondary support to Site Senior Executive and Weighbridge Controller in his/her absence.
Other duties as directed or required within your field of competency.

QUALIFICATIONS
Competencies recognised by the Mining Safety and Health Advisory Committee (MQSHA 1999 and MQSHR 2017):
RIIRIS301E Apply risk management processes
RIIWHS301E Conduct safety and health investigations
RIICOM301E Communicate information

SKILLS
Proven hands on experience in fixed and mobile crushing/screening and quarrying.
Strong mechanical aptitude.
Proven leadership qualities as well as a strong commitment to safety.
Self-motivated, hardworking, reliable and a team player.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Computer skills including MS Office products.

PERFORMANCE GOALS
Ensure all workers are working both efficiently and safely at all times.
Interact with Management and other Employees with a friendly manner at all times.

ACHIEVE Group Australia

Posted 16m ago
Exciting 12-Month Site Supervisor Opportunity - Mine Remediation Projects

Are you an experienced site supervisor ready to lead critical remediation projects across a diverse portfolio of abandoned mine sites? Our client, a leading government department, is seeking a motivated and safety-focused Site Supervisor to manage day-to-day operations on a 5/2 roster, 50 hours per week.

In this pivotal role, you will oversee crews, contractors, and site activities to ensure the safe and efficient delivery of essential mine remediation and water management initiatives. If you thrive in a fast-paced, solutions-oriented environment and excel at leading teams, this could be your next career-defining opportunity.

Key Responsibilities:
- Supervise day-to-day site activities and care & maintenance operations
- Oversee crews of operators and site support staff
- Manage and coordinate contractors completing specialized work
- Conduct site inductions, risk assessments, inspections, and safety audits
- Identify, mitigate, and escalate safety risks and operational concerns
- Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency and safety

To Succeed, You'll Need:
- Demonstrated experience in project supervision, particularly within mining or construction
- A trade qualification or tertiary degree in a relevant technical field 
- Excellent communication skills and a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset
- Current C Class Driver's Licence
- Project management and risk management experience or qualifications 
- Flexibility to travel or work outside standard hours as required

This 12-month contract offers an attractive remuneration package and the opportunity to make a real impact on critical environmental remediation projects. If you're ready to take the next step in your career, we encourage you to apply now.

To explore this exciting opportunity, please call 1300 129 732 or visit www.achievegroup.com.au
raw_texts.Anglo Dragline.docx:
Mining Supervisor: Dragline
Anglo American
3.4117 reviews·View all jobs
Moura, Gladstone & Central QLD
Mining - Operations 
Full time
Competitive Salary + 12% Super + Annual Bonus
Posted 5d ago
Dedicated to excellence? Join us where we've got your back every step of the way.
Dawson Mine
Steelmaking coal is an essential ingredient in blast-furnace steel manufacture, producing steel used for critical building and infrastructure around the world.  In Australia, we operate five steelmaking coal mines in Queensland’s Bowen Basin, and have additional joint venture interests in steelmaking coal and manganese, as well as copper exploration projects underway in North West Queensland and Western Australia.
Dawson is located at the southern end of the Bowen Basin in Central Queensland, being just over a 2hrs drive west from either Rockhampton or Gladstone, or 4.5hrs drive north of the Darling Downs or South Burnett.
Job Description
Dawson Mine has an exciting opportunity for an experienced Mining Supervisor: Dragline to join the Production team on a permanent basis, based in Moura, QLD. This role will have you oversee and supervise a team of Dragline Operators operating our BE1370 and Marion 8200 Draglines to produce Steelmaking Coal.

You will be responsible for:
Coordination of personnel, equipment and resources within Dragline operations.
Ensure site dragline activities are undertaken in a safe, cost effective and efficient manner in line with agreed operational standards and targets working as one team.
Enforce plan compliance and promote engineer and operator engagement to ensure that the business can meet production targets.
Ensure compliance with statutory legislative requirements.
Qualifications
You will want to show exceptional communication and people skills, possessing knowledge and experience with an ability to build relationships and work in a team environment. This is your opportunity to join a team of passionate and supportive industry leading experts contributing to a positive culture of continuous improvement!
To be successful for this position, you will ideally hold:
Supervisory experience within an open-cut coal mine .
Operational dragline experience is highly advantageous.
Hold the Supervisor & Risk Facilitator competencies  is desirable.
Significant operational experience within the coal mining industry.
A strong understanding of open cut mining equipment and mining techniques.
A desire to continually work at being an effective supervisor and leader.
Additional information
5/4/4/5 Lifestyle Roster
Industry leading remuneration and attractive yearly bonus
Residential, DIDO or company funded FIFO options available
Company funded camp and subsidised housing available
Relocation Assistance available
External education assistance, novated leasing, private health discounts, additional Anglo discounts
Gym/Health incentive + On site Physio + On site EAP
What we Offer
When you join Anglo American, you can expect to enjoy a competitive salary and benefits package. But more than this, you’ll find yourself in an environment where the opportunities for learning and growth are second to none. From technical training to leadership programmes, we bring out the best in our people. There are plenty of opportunities to move onwards and upwards too. We’re a large, successful multinational company – and we’re still growing all the time.
Who we are
We aim to lead the industry by pursuing ever safer and more responsible ways of working, demonstrating integrity, and showing care and respect for people and the planet. That means we are constantly seeking new opportunities to mine and process our products sustainably, using less water, less energy and more precise extraction technologies.  As the custodians of coal and other precious natural resources – diamonds , copper, platinum and other precious metals, iron ore and nickel – our extraordinary teams work safely and collaboratively, with the utmost consideration for local communities, our customers and the world at large.

Background checks
Successful candidates will be required to complete background screening which may include a criminal check and validation of qualifications.

Inclusion and Diversity
At Anglo American, we embrace change and promote an inclusive and diverse environment where you can bring your whole self to work, every day. We are adopting new approaches to recruitment, talent development, mentoring, parental leave, and flexible working to further support diversity across our business.

How we are committed to your safety
Nothing is more important to us than ensuring you return home safely after a day’s work. To make that happen, we have the most rigorous safety standards in the industry. Not only that, we’re also continually investing in new technologies – from drones to data analytics – that are helping to make mining safer.
How to apply
To apply for this role, please complete our online application form. You will have the opportunity to upload your CV and other relevant documentation as part of this process.
REF77327T
CLOSING DATE 
25 March 2026
Please note, screening and interviews may take place prior to closing date – applications may also close before this date.
Recruitment Agencies
Anglo American Steelmaking Coal does not accept unsolicited applications from Recruitment Agencies.

Posted 5d ago

BHP is a world-leading resources company. We work in more than 90
locations worldwide and our products are sold globally.\
\
We\'ve positioned our business to support the megatrends shaping our
world. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global
infrastructure and the energy transition. Copper for renewable energy.
And we are moving into potash to support more sustainable farming.\
\
A resource mix for today - and critical to the future.

About BHP

At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and
reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a
diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career
opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work
environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values
and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our
success.

About BHP Operations Services

BHP Operations Services delivers industry leading mining services across
BHP operations Australia-wide with specialist capabilities in load and
haul, fixed plant maintenance and mobile maintenance.

About the Roles

BHP is seeking motivated and safety-focused leaders to join our
Operations Services team in various leadership positions, including
Supervisor and Superintendent roles. These positions play a vital role
in driving team performance, maintaining safety excellence, and
supporting continuous improvement across our operations.

You\'ll be based at one of our Queensland sites --- Saraji, Caval Ridge,
Goonyella Riverside, or Peak Downs --- depending on operational
requirements and your area of expertise.

-   Supervisor positions: 7 days on, 7 days off, 7 nights on, 7 nights
    off roster

-   Superintendent positions: 5 days on, 2 days off roster

As part of our leadership team, you\'ll be responsible for supporting
and empowering your team to deliver outstanding results while fostering
an inclusive, respectful, and high‑performing culture.

About You

You\'re a people‑focused leader who thrives on mentoring and developing
others. You value safety above all, communicate effectively, and build
strong working relationships across all levels of the business. Your
background in a similar operational or leadership environment will
enable you to contribute meaningfully to our team\'s success.

Key Accountabilities:

-   Lead and support your team to achieve operational targets safely and
    efficiently

-   Foster a positive and productive culture built on trust, respect,
    and accountability

-   Promote and uphold BHP\'s HSEC standards, including proactive hazard
    management

-   Identify and implement opportunities to improve performance,
    processes, and systems

-   Collaborate cross‑functionally to support continuous improvement and
    business outcomes

-   Drive asset reliability and operational efficiency through effective
    maintenance strategies

-   Coach, mentor, and develop team members to reach their full
    potential

-   Utilise systems such as SAP to plan, manage, and report on
    operational performance

-   A trade qualification or relevant tertiary qualification is highly
    desirable, along with demonstrated leadership experience in mining,
    maintenance, or other heavy industry sectors.

Why BHP?

You\'ll be part of a company that values its people, putting safety and
inclusion at the forefront of everything we do. Working alongside
supportive leaders, you\'ll have the opportunity to build your
leadership capability, drive business improvement, and create a

pathway for continued career growth within BHP.

Be part of something bigger --- join us in shaping the future of our
operations.

About Our Process

At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP
Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the
recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted
to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police /
criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due
diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your
BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.

Supporting a Diverse Workforce

The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides
significant opportunity for professional development, but also
attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a
best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of
our employees\' lives that are important, and work is only one of these,
so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These
benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental
leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms. 

At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal
Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and
inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every
day. We are focused on creating a workforce that\'s more diverse and
represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work
environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with
respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true
diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual
orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more
aspects of your identity.\
\
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair,
equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that
it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for
your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone
about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us
at inclusion@bhp.com.

Come and be a part of this success.
About the Role
BHP Technology is looking for a Supervisor for our Technology Site Operations team to work at our Infrastructure & Services location in Moranbah Queensland, supporting both the Coal Mining Act and Workplace Health and Safety as the regulatory bodies.
As OT Supervisor, you will be responsible for the preventative maintenance and technical field support provided for all of the Village and Office Infrastructure and SASH controls utilising Operational & Information Technology Systems. The role is a Full Time position with BHP on a DIDO/Residential role on a Lifestyle  days only roster working 12 hour shifts.
Reporting to the Superintendent Connectivity & Infrastructure - Site Applications + Operations, your key accountabilities will include:
Site Responsible Person for Technology resources on site  and ensures your team works within Safe Systems of Work in a changing work environment
Manages and coordinates tasks and resources available on site to ensure incidents and events can be resolved in accordance with the business priorities/expectations
Review Maintenance work tasks/schedules and work with planners ensuring good work management practices are followed through computer based management system  and 
Collaborate with other departments  to ensure the effective operation and maintenance of the technology environment to support business systems/operations including: desktops, network infrastructure, wireless telemetry, CCTV, Physical access management systems and videoconferencing
Works with the Mineral Australia Coal Technology Delivery Team representatives, Project Managers and I&S Engineering to facilitate site-based project activities to ensure safe execution of work and minimal disruption to operations
Acts as the escalation contact for Technology site operations issues supporting Minerals Australia Technology SASH controls, and Service Owner accountabilities across all of the above locations
Contractor representative for associated contractors supporting both Service Owner and I&S work scoping and compliance activities.
About You
To be successful in this role.
You will have a background in Operations Technology in large-scale environments, preferably mining.
You are likely to be multi-skilled and have knowledge of mining operations technology; security systems, IT and OT networks, Wireless technology , CCTV and Key Management, SALTO security locks.
A trade certificate / tertiary qualification in Telecommunications, Electrical, Electronics or similar discipline, is a mandatory requirement for this role.
You will be a great people manager, leading a team of 2-5 personnel, with proven experience leading high performing team in an operational environment.
As the preferred candidate, you will have outstanding interpersonal, communication and presentation skills, with the ability to work with diverse stakeholder groups at all levels.
Experience of SAP Work Management, Standardised Work, BHP Operating System and cost-control will be well regarded.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to prioritise effectively in a high-pressure environment.
You can interpret and read maintenance plans and technical information. Proven Safety leadership skills and a C class driver’s licence are required for this position.

At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. Please note as part of this recruitment process the successful applicant will need to conduct a Queensland legislated coal mine workers’ health assessment.
From 1 April 2023, ResHealth will be mandatory for the completion of coal mine workers’ health assessments, allowing you to submit, monitor and review the progress of your health assessment in a secure online environment. For more information please go to the ResHealth website.

These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.

We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. About the Role

Reporting to the Project Superintendent, the Lead Supervisor is
responsible for the safe, effective and timely execution of project and
shutdown activities. This role provides frontline leadership to
supervisors and work crews, ensuring work is delivered safely, in
compliance with BMA standards, and aligned to agreed schedules and
milestones.

The role acts as the on scene escalation point during execution,
coordinating work fronts, managing emergent work, and supporting
continuous improvement in safety, productivity and communication.

This position is based at the CQO and will be offered on an 8/6 dayshift
roster during shutdowns and 5/2 M-F between shutdowns with FILO as an
option.

Key responsibilities:

-   Lead and support supervisors and execution teams to deliver work
    safely, to plan and within schedule.


-   Manage work fronts by escalating safety, cost or schedule risks as
    required.


-   Set and communicate 24 hour priorities aligned to shutdown and
    project objectives.


-   Manage emergent work, ensuring approvals are obtained and scope is
    captured in the schedule.


-   Track and update schedule progress, attend schedule reviews, and
    develop short term look ahead plans.


-   Ensure work packs, permits and handovers are completed and signed
    off across all work fronts.


-   Coordinate pad planning, work-front readiness and labour allocation
    in line with parts and resource availability.


-   Support contractor performance, approvals and timesheet sign offs.


-   Ensure all work is executed in accordance with BMA SHMS, permits and
    critical risk controls.


-   Supervise high risk activities including lifts and isolations,
    confirming task readiness prior to execution.


-   Respond to injuries and incidents, support medical response and
    return to work processes.


-   Support incident investigations and ensure corrective actions are
    identified and closed out.


-   Maintain safety documentation, registers and reporting requirements.


-   Maintain delay registers and communicate impacts to schedule
    delivery.


-   Support toolbox talks, shift handovers and operational briefings to
    ensure clear communication across crews.


-   Identify and implement opportunities to improve safety, schedule
    efficiency and execution outcomes.


-   Participate in root cause analysis and continuous improvement
    initiatives.


-   Capture and communicate lessons learned during execution.

About You

-   Experience supervising or coordinating work in construction,
    maintenance or heavy industry environments.


-   Strong leadership, organisational and communication skills.


-   Demonstrated commitment to mine site safety systems and field
    leadership.


-   Proficiency in Microsoft Office .


-   Desirable


-   Exposure to shutdown or mining project environments.


-   Understanding of scheduling and work management systems .


-   Trade qualification or Engineering background in Electrical,
    Mechanical or Structural Engineering


-   Dragline, Rope Shovel or large mobile equipment experience.

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your

Full job description
Requisition ID: 3380
Department: Operations
Lead from the front as a Production Supervisor at Meandu Mine—drive safety, performance, and team success in a dynamic, residential-based role.
About Us:
BUMA's vision is to build a modern mining and rehabilitation company that drives growth. BUMA Australia adopts a relationship-focused, long-term strategy for project success. We consistently deliver value to clients throughout all mining stages, leveraging expertise and equipment for comprehensive surface operation services.
About the Role:
Join our established team at Meandu as a Production Supervisor and take the lead in driving operational performance. In this key role, you'll use your leadership skills to create a strong safety culture by setting a positive example and making proactive decisions. Your ability to prioritise tasks and support your team will be key to ensuring a safe and productive workplace. If you're ready to make a real impact and work with a highly experienced team we want to hear from you!
Please note – Meandu is a Residential Mine – no camp accommodation or housing provided.
Key Responsibilities:
Oversee the daily operations of the mine, ensuring safe and efficient production in line with Mine Safety and Inspection requirements.
Lead and manage personnel and equipment to optimise production activities and meet operational targets.
Effectively allocate tasks and manage workloads within the production team to achieve development goals and maintain project timelines.
Ensure full compliance with the Mine Safety and Inspection Act and Regulations, fostering a strong safety culture and proactive risk management.
About You:
5+ years of production experience in an open-cut coal mining operation, with a strong understanding of site operations and efficiencies.
2+ years of supervisory experience in an open-cut coal mining environment, demonstrating effective leadership and team management.
In-depth knowledge of QLD Coal Mining legislation, ensuring compliance and best practices in safety and operations.
Current C Class Driver’s Licence.
A proven ability to motivate teams, drive performance, and achieve business targets in a dynamic mining environment.
S1,2,3 and G2 Certificate.
Working at BUMA:
BUMA Australia is a people focused business, with a strong focus on safety, training, development and continuous improvement. As a BUMA employee at Meandu you will have access to;
Family-friendly lifestyle roster  – day and night rotating.
Full-time, permanent role.
Annual pay reviews.
Training and development opportunities within a diverse and supportive environment.
Employee financial, lifestyle and health benefits such as discounted health cover, discounted car rentals, superannuation, novated leasing and an Employee Assistance Program.
Meandu Mine
BUMA Australia provides a total mine service to Stanwell Corporation’s Meandu Mine to deliver coal to the adjacent Tarong power station. We are responsible for both managing and operating the mine.
Services provided include planning, drilling, overburden removal, coal mining, coal processing, rehabilitation, and maintenance of mobile and fixed plant. The project uses a combination of dragline and truck-and shovel operations. Meandu Mine currently has five working pits, with sufficient coal to fuel the stations until at least 2037.
To be considered for this opportunity please "Apply Now" to submit your application!
All final applicants for this position will be asked to consent to a police check. Please note that people with criminal records are not automatically barred from applying for this position. Each application will be considered on its merits. For further information on careers at BUMA please visit our website https://buma.com.au/careers/why-join-us/

Bechtel

Full job description

Requisition ID: 289502

-   Relocation Authorized: National - Camp

-   Telework Type: Full-Time Office/Project

-   Work Location: Cloncurry, QLD

\
Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:

Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects
in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown
economies, improved the resiliency of the world\'s infrastructure,
increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the
world a safer, cleaner place.

Differentiated by the quality of our people and our relentless drive to
deliver the most successful outcomes, we align our capabilities to our
customers\' objectives to create a lasting positive impact. We serve the
Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security & Environmental; Energy; Mining &
Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span
from initial planning and investment, through start-up and operations.

Core to Bechtel is our Vision, Values and Commitments. They are what we
believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver. Learn more about
our extraordinary teams building inspiring projects in our Impact
Report.

Project Overview:

Bechtel Mining and Metals offers engineering, procurement, and
construction services for customers in the mining sector across multiple
commodities including Iron Ore, Bauxite, Alumina, Aluminium, Copper,
Gold, Coal, Lithium and Rare Earths markets.The M&M global business unit
also has offices in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide in Australia along with
execution units in the United States, Peru, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia,
India and other project locations around the world.

Job Summary:

The Supervisor for site services is responsible for supervising and
directing operations within the in directs and site support services for
construction. Supervises and directs temporary facility and indirect
craft professionals, subordinate contractor services, and others, as
assigned. Ensures assigned construction performance is following
specifications and within schedules and budgets.

\
This role is offered on a FIFO basis to North Queensland, operating on a
2:1 roster

Major Responsibilities:

-   Implementing the project ES&H program and quality control program.

-   Supervises, coordinates and/or directs craft activities as assigned
    for the services disciplines.

-   Coordinating craft safety training and always enforcing safe work
    practices in conjunction with the ES&H staff.

-   Planning the craft manpower and interfacing with Industrial
    Relations for craft recruitment, hiring, and skills training
    programs.

-   Oversight of the needs of the Site services crews for craft
    personnel, subcontractor support, construction services,
    construction equipment, tools, and consumables as well as optimizing
    their utilization.

-   Working closely with other lead discipline superintendents,
    supervisors and coordinating efforts and activities to meet the
    overall project requirements.

-   Maintaining a high level of housekeeping throughout the work area.

Education and Experience Requirements:

-   10 years of related large industrial construction project services
    support experience.

-   5 years of supervisory experience in a site services function role
    with experience in managing and maintain temporary facilities, waste
    management, traffic control and road and site access.

Required Knowledge and Skills:

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of construction operations at
    the jobsite

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of the requirements of the
    Bechtel Environmental, Safety & Health  Core Processes and
    Bechtel's "Zero Accidents" philosophy.

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of Bechtel\'s Standard Work
    Process Procedures .

-   Must have knowledge and understanding of Bechtel Standard
    Application Program  standards and other automation tools
    which are used on Bechtel projects.

-   Must be computer literate in word processing, spreadsheet, and
    database applications in accordance with current BSAP standards.

-   Must be familiar with current industry quality standards and
    applicable code requirements.

Total Rewards/Benefits:

For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of
employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world\'s
toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people
thrive. Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance
our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to
build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total
Rewards

Diverse teams build the extraordinary:

As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude
of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This
diversity has made us a more trusted partner, more effective problem
solvers and innovators, and a more attractive destination for leading
talent.

We are committed to being a company where every colleague feels that
they belong-where colleagues feel part of \"One Team,\" respected and
rewarded for what they bring, supported in pursuing their goals,
invested in our values and purpose, and treated equitably.

\
Bechtel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will
receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color,
religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, age,
national origin, disability, citizenship status , protected veteran status, genetic information, and any other
characteristic protected by federal, state or local law. Applicants with
a disability, who require a reasonable accommodation for any part of the
application or hiring process, may e-mail their request
to acesstmt@bechtel.com

Our services span from initial planning and investment, through start-up and operations.
Core to Bechtel is our Vision, Values and Commitments. They are what we believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver. Learn more about our extraordinary teams building inspiring projects in our Impact Report.
Project Overview:
Bechtel helps customers deliver projects of purpose that create a lasting positive legacy globally. These large, complex, and multi-disciplined projects create jobs, grow economies, and provide opportunities for local and regional communities.
The Eva Copper Mine Project is a greenfield resource poised to become the largest new mine in Queensland, Australia. It is located in Cloncurry on the traditional lands of the Kalkadoon people. The Project is owned by Harmony, a world-class gold and copper mining and development company with assets in South Africa, Australia, and Papua New Guinea.
Bechtel is delivering the Engineering, Procurement and Construction  scope for the Eva Copper Process Plant and Non-Process Infrastructure. Bechtel is providing these EPC services across all disciplines and functions for this exciting capital project.
Job Summary:
In this role, you will liaise between engineering and field operations and provide guidance on executing constructability plans. You will lead construction design decisions, interdisciplinary reviews, and project staffing. You will monitor construction performance and review deliverables. You will use your expertise to improve Bechtel’s existing methods by initiating project assessments and implementing improvements.
This role is offered on a FIFO basis to North Queensland, operating on a 2:1 roster
Major Responsibilities:
Supervises and/or directs craft activities as assigned.
Schedules work to be performed from 3-week lookahead scaffold requests and reviews work accomplished by each craft.
Performs general functions inherent in all supervisory field positions. .
Coordinates established area operational plans.
Coordinates activities with other disciplines or areas and clients’ resident representatives, as instructed by construction management.
Ensures the most current design document is used to complete the work and that all design changes are approved prior to implementation.
Ensures assigned construction operations and subcontractors’ performance are in compliance with specifications and within schedules and budgets.
Ensures the implementation of corporate/project policies, procedures, and instructions within assigned operations.
Education and Experience Requirements:
Requires 5-8 years of relevant scaffolding industrial project experience or 9 -12 years of relevant scaffolding work experience
Required Knowledge and Skills:
Knowledge of a single or multi-discipline construction or engineering operation.
Prior experience on construction projects with knowledge of supervisory functions relating to field activities.
Strong analytical and judicious planning: able to prioritize and balance short-term and long-term priorities.
Knowledge of the use of subcontracts, and the contractor and subcontractor’s responsibilities contained in subcontract documents.
Experience working with, supervising and administering subcontractors and/or Direct Hire workforce.
Knowledge and understanding of the application and requirements of the Project Environmental, Safety & Health.
Knowledge of project-specific environmental compliance requirements.
Total Rewards/Benefits:
For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world's toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people thrive. Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total Rewards
Diverse teams build the extraordinary:
As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This diversity has made us a more trusted partner, more effective problem solvers and innovators, and a more attractive destination for leading talent.
We are committed to being a company where every colleague feels that they belong-where colleagues feel part of "One Team," respected and rewarded for what they bring, supported in pursuing their goals, invested in our values and purpose, and treated equitably.

About the job
Job description:
About us
From humble beginnings in 2011, Evolution Mining is now a leading gold producer, operating six mines across Australia and Canada. Enacting our vision to be inspired people creating a premier global gold company, we are indeed a bold team with a bright future. Our operational diversity offers our employees access to many experiences and career opportunities within the one company. So, whether you choose to work at one of our underground or open pit operations, explore for our future as part of our Discovery team, or join our Group Office, we know you’ll have a bright future with us.
At Mungari
At Mungari, now with a mine life through to 2038, our people have a longer future with opportunities to grow. With underground and open pit mining, and an expansion of the processing plant completed, the operational diversity and unique work experience is great for building and uplifting careers. Located in the Western Australia Goldfields, you can choose what suits your priorities. Work an attractive fly-in/fly out  roster from Perth or live local and enjoy our close-knit communities and local lifestyles.
Your Benefits
At Evolution, we support, develop and reward everyone who chooses to be a part of our bright future. Your benefits include relocation assistance, salary packaging , life insurance and income protection, quarterly performance bonuses, employee share scheme, referral bonuses, recognition program, study assistance, discounted health insurance and local employee benefits.
Choose an 8:6 roster, FIFO from Perth or live local in the Goldfields and enjoy genuine community connection.
About the role
Reporting to the Underground Manager, the Underground Mining Superintendent is accountable for the safe, efficient and cost-effective delivery of underground mining production in alignment with the Short Range Forecast , budget, and Evolution’s safety and operational standards. You will translate strategy into executable plans, ensuring underground activities are planned, resourced and delivered safely, while developing the leadership capability of Supervisors and the broader crew.
You will remove systemic roadblocks, provide strong safety leadership, and drive performance outcomes across development, production and services to achieve tonnes, metres and compliance targets. Key Responsibilities
Champion a high-performance safety culture, ensuring full compliance with site and statutory requirements.
Proactively identify and control hazards, supported by regular underground engagement and visible safety leadership.
Lead incident investigations and drive lasting corrective actions.
Deliver the Short Range Forecast  and achieve production targets safely and efficiently.
Oversee development, production, ground support, paste fill and services to ensure seamless operational execution.
Optimise productivity through improved cycle times, equipment performance and effective resource allocation.
Partner with Technical Services, Geology and Geotechnical teams to deliver practical, executable short-term plans.
Build leadership capability by coaching and developing Supervisors while managing workforce planning and performance.
Drive continuous improvement by removing operational barriers and enhancing safety, cost and productivity outcomes.
Provide clear, timely reporting and maintain strong communication across operational and support teams.
About you
You are an experienced underground mining leader with a strong operational mindset and the ability to drive safe, efficient and disciplined execution. Your experience enables you to make informed decisions, influence outcomes and develop the capability of your team. You build culture through your leadership — balancing accountability with coaching, clarity and support.
10+ years’ mining experience, including 5+ years in a supervisory or leadership role.
Underground Supervisor’s Certificate .
First Class Mine Manager’s Certificate — or working towards it .
Extensive operational experience in underground mining, with strong knowledge of legislation and safety systems.
Demonstrated success leading multidisciplinary underground teams.
Strong capability in planning, cost management and operational execution.
Competence in Pronto, Word and Excel; confident in coaching, feedback and team development.
C-Class Manual Driver’s Licence.
At Evolution, as our name suggests, change and transformation is in our DNA. We want the Evolution experience to be your career highlight. We are an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a more inclusive and diverse workforce that represents the communities in which we operate. We provide a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all and candidates from all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. We are focused on keeping our people safe and healthy and creating sustainable value in an environmentally and socially responsible way. Join our bold team to start your next exciting chapter.

At Ernest Henry, our mine life extension to 2040 and a doubling of ore reserves offers more operational diversity on site, and a bright future for our people and the community.

At Evolution, we support, develop and reward everyone who chooses to be a part of our bright future. Glencore Mount Isa Mines is currently seeking a Supervisor Services within our Operations Services team, reporting to the Superintendent Services. In this role you will be required to effectively co-ordinate and supervise the personnel as they complete the shift activities at the Zinc Filter plant Operation asset.

Mount Isa
Mount Isa is a thriving city acting as a hub for regional business, transport, services and government in North West Queensland. Mount Isa Mines attracts the best people from all over Australia and the world who are drawn to working for a high-calibre mining operation with the added benefit of a comfortable residential-based work arrangement.
We believe in the value of our people and our approach fosters the highest level of professionalism, ownership and entrepreneurial spirit in all our people while never compromising on their safety and well-being. We offer excellent remuneration package and benefits including and employee rewards program, above market superannuation, and significant professional development opportunities. Learn more about Mount Isa Mines at www.mountisamines.com.au

Mount Isa Mines location and roster offers an attractive lifestyle option. You will always have something to do, go to restaurants, movies, join a gym and sporting activities, play golf, as well as fishing and camping and activities at the lake.

The Role
As the Supervisor - Services, you will be responsible for ensuring all tasks and duties are completed safely, efficiently, and on time in support of Surface Operations. This role is not limited to the following and includes as an example - the day-to-day management of wet and dry reagents, media control, and housekeeping support across multiple plant assets. You will oversee the coordination and operation of employee transportation between the Maydown CICO changehouse facility and the Zinc Concentrator, manage third-party Lead concentrate handling activities, and ensure high standards of housekeeping within the concentrator administration buildings.
This is a residential position, and you will be required to work a 5/2, 4/3 9-hour day roster.

The successful candidate will be responsible for:
Manage a team of approximately 10 employees and contractors
Ensuring reagent is available for both concentrators
Mill media addition and management for the zinc concentrator
You will have the ability to make decisions, work to strict deadlines and develop ongoing contingency lans for continuous improvement
Ownership and management of a small fleet of vehicles and equipment including forklifts, loaders, excavator and bobcats
Take ownership and accountability to meet agreed targets in a manner consistent with our values
Provide mentoring to and effectively communicate with your crew
Be safety focused and prepared to work with others to achieve daily targets and outcomes while continuing to drive a safe, productive and effective workplace where teams are empowered and supported to make improvements
Oversee and maintain compliance for the services crew with safety and training
You will have the following skillset and attributes:
Previous experience with frontline leadership
A minimum of 5 years working in the operational side of the mining industry
The ability to lead by example and a strong work ethic
A genuine interest in leading safe teams
Experience in budget control, Microsoft Office Suite, Ellipse, SAP and/or similar software packages
Technical knowledge in Filtration plant operations
Possess a high level of organisational skills will ensure you thrive in this fantastic opportunity for career progression

For 100 years, Mount Isa has been a thriving city, acting as the hub for regional business, transport, services and government in North West Queensland. The city offers an array of leisure activities, from dining and cinema to outdoor adventures like fishing and camping. With Mount Isa Mines, you get the best of both worlds – a thriving career and a fulfilling lifestyle.

Mount Isa Mines 
Mount Isa Mines is one of the world's largest mining complexes. We operate mining and processing streams, copper and zinc-lead-silver, to deliver natural resources of enduring value in modern society.

At Mount Isa Mines, we believe in our people. We cultivate an environment of professionalism, ownership, and innovation while prioritizing safety and well-being. Our employee rewards program, market-leading superannuation, and commitment to professional development set us apart. Learn more about Mount Isa Mines at www.mountisamines.com.au 

The Role
You will be responsible for leadership and supervision of your team and management of multiple tasks across all areas of the business, this position will see you bring people together by encouraging input from key stakeholders and leading the way with a strong understanding of our vision for the future. You will be a positive driver of change and have the ability to effectively communicate across all work groups. As a safety leader in our business, you will strive to foster safety citizenship within our business and actively promote and support the Glencore Values. 

To be the successful candidate you will have, previous supervisory experience, excellent interpersonal and organisational skills. A strong proven focus on Reliability and improvements, A sound awareness of the MIM surface lease operations and extensive knowledge of multiple light vehicles makes and models.

Your key responsibilities include:
Responsible for communicating accurate and relevant information and insight to line management on daily performance and against the weekly schedule
Responsible for ensuring shift handover is successfully managed
Responsible for proactively assessing and providing input and intelligence to asset planning on equipment to improve availability 
Responsible on a daily basis for compliance to the weekly schedule to achieve established KPIs and compliance with forecast costs
Responsible for providing visible leadership and ensuring compliance to GFM policies, standards and procedures for the Maintenance Services team
Responsible for the optimization of assigned resources and continuous improvement to minimize cost and waste
The successful applicants will have:
Trade Qualified
S1,2,3
Thorough knowledge of asset maintenance methodologies
Working knowledge of contemporary equipment
Demonstrated experience in Light Vehicle Repair Industry.
5 years underground mining experience
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite- Word, Excel and Outlook
Demonstrated knowledge of risk management, safety systems and legislative requirements for mining operations
Experience supervising and developing large work teams to deliver business outcome
Competitive Remuneration packaged with 13% superannuation;
Relocation package inclusive of 12 weeks furnished transit accommodation;
Benefits program including discounts with Health Insurance providers, online and in-store discounts through
Glencore Rewards, salary sacrificing vehicles, Employee Referral Program and Recognition and Reward programs;
Fantastic professional development opportunities with Professional External Education Assistance, leadership development programs, ambassador Programs and more; and unrivalled career opportunities.
Residential Allowance

Production Supervisor | Carmichael Mine 
If you’re a hands-on leader who thrives in fast-paced environments, this is your chance to step into a pivotal role overseeing production in a large-scale open-cut coal operation. Backed by long-term project stability and a strong safety culture, you’ll lead from the front while continuing to grow your career.

What’s in it for you?
A permanent position on a long-life mining project. You’ll enjoy a balanced 7/7 roster, paid FIFO from Townsville or Rockhampton, and the convenience of a site where camp, pit and airport are all just minutes apart. Add in fully catered accommodation, gym and recreational facilities, and you’ve got a setup designed to make your swing as smooth as possible.

About the role
You’ll be responsible for leading production crews to deliver safe, efficient, and high-performing outcomes on site. This is a role for someone who can balance people leadership with operational delivery—keeping safety front of mind while driving productivity and team performance.

Day-to-day, you’ll be:
Overseeing and coordinating production activities to meet targets
Leading pre-starts and reinforcing a strong safety-first culture
Ensuring compliance with site safety, environmental, and statutory requirements
Coaching and mentoring crew members to build capability and engagement
Monitoring performance and identifying opportunities for improvement
Maintaining accurate production and operational reporting
About you
You’re an experienced Supervisor in open-cut mining who knows how to get the best out of your team while maintaining high safety standards. You’re adaptable, solutions-focused, and confident making decisions in a dynamic environment.

To be successful, you’ll bring:
Standard 11 and relevant Supervisor competencies 
Proven experience leading crews in a production mining environment
Strong knowledge of WHS legislation and site compliance requirements
A genuine commitment to safety and continuous improvement
Clear communication skills and the ability to influence and develop others
Solid computer skills, including Microsoft Office
If you’re ready to step into a role where you can make an impact and be part of a long-term mining operation, apply now and join the MacKellar team.

Supervisor - Production
Full job description
Production Supervisor - Mt Carlton
Macmahon is seeking an experienced Production Supervisor to support the recommencement of operations at Mt Carlton mine. Located approximately two hours south of Townsville, Mt Carlton is a gold silver operation preparing to transition from care and maintenance back into production. This restart presents a unique opportunity to contribute to the establishment of safe, efficient and sustainable operational practices from the outset.
This position operates on a 7 Days on / 7 Days Off roster, followed by 7 Nights on / 7 Days off roster, DIDO Only.
About The Role
Lead safe work practices by driving a proactive safety culture and ensuring full compliance with site standards.
Supervise daily mining operations across digging, hauling and dumping activities to deliver safe and efficient outcomes.
Execute the mine plan, ensuring production activities align with short term schedules and operational targets.
Monitor fleet performance, including cycle times, delays and equipment utilisation, optimising outcomes in collaboration with Dispatch.
Provide leadership and coaching to operators to build capability, maintain engagement and support performance.
Ensure accurate reporting through shift logs, production data, environmental checks and incident documentation.
Coordinate with site departments, including Maintenance, Technical Services and Drill & Blast, to maintain operational flow.
About You
Extensive surface mining experience as a Production Supervisor, Leading Hand or Senior Operator
A strong, proven commitment to safety and excellent communication skills across all levels.
Background and technical knowledge of a range of equipment.
Mechanical knowledge to a level of understanding equipment capabilities, maintenance scheduling and preventative maintenance methods.
Detailed knowledge of mining and safety regulations.
Certification in Frontline Management.
Employee Benefits
Ongoing career development opportunities
Access to lifestyle, financial, and mental health support programs
Salary sacrifice options for flights, vehicles, airport parking and more
Discounts across Macmahon's retail, vehicle, insurance, and banking partners
About Macmahon
Macmahon is a leading Australian contract mining company with projects throughout Australia and Southeast Asia. Delivering a comprehensive range of surface and underground mining, civil design and construction, performance enhancement, and mine site maintenance and rehabilitation services to the resources sector.
Recruitment process
Successful applicants must pass a pre-employment medical, including drug and alcohol screening, as well as criminal history and qualification checks. Proof of working rights in Australia  is also required.
Macmahon is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages suitably qualified women and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islanders to apply.
Macmahon Winning Values

Company description:
Workplace Type: Onsite
At Newmont, people create the value in the metals we mine and are ambassadors in the communities where they live and work. As a team, we foster an environment in support of our people vision, where everyone belongs, thrives and is valued. We have built a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can bring their differences to support delivering results together.
Newmont Tanami is on Aboriginal freehold land 540km northwest of Alice Springs. It is a long-life, low-cost, world-class asset in a core Newmont region. The Tanami Expansion 2 project, aims to extend the mine life beyond 2040, investing in a hoisting shaft which will increase the production of the underground mine from 2.6 Mtpa to 3.2 Mtpa. Both the successful exploration within the underground operation and implementation of the first Tanami Expansion has laid the foundation to further develop the operation to remain a major contributor to the Newmont portfolio. A fully equipped camp is located 40km from the site offering leisure facilities for those flying in from Perth, Brisbane, Alice Springs or Darwin.
Join us and unearth your potential in a global company that is at the forefront of shaping the future of the mining industry.

We employ approximately 500 people, who are our most valuable asset and we invest in developing our management and leadership talent throughout our operations.

Yancoal Australia is Australia's largest pure play coal producer. If you are looking for an opportunity to join a great team and have previous experience Supervising in the Mining industry, we would love to hear from you!
Job Tasks and Responsibilities
The Production Supervisor, reporting to the Production Superintendent, will be responsible for the management and oversight of a production crew in conjunction with an OCE.
This includes ensuring the safe execution of the mine plan in accordance with operating standards, procedures, and defined processes to deliver optimal production outcomes.
In this role your key responsibilities will include:
Ensuring compliance with the requirements of the Mine Safety and Inspection Act and Mines Safety Inspection Regulations
Providing direct supervision of employees and sub-contractors working within the area of responsibility and allocating day-to-day work priorities
Focus on creating a culture of care by being a visible safety leader, engaging, developing and coaching your team
Managing workloads and allocating duties across the production team to deliver the plan and ensure all development targets are achieved
Promoting continuous improvement by challenging work practices and encouraging employee participation in new concepts
Skills and Experience
The ideal candidate will have the proven ability to lead positive change and support the continual development of a high performing team, dedicated to working safely, complying with all site safety standards, mine working procedures and ensure a safe and environmentally healthy workplace.
In addition, the ideal candidate will meet the following:
Thorough understanding of individual responsibilities in relation to the Coal Mining Safety & Health Act and associated regulations
Well-developed written and verbal communication skills
Ability to create positive working relationships and be adaptable to various communication styles
Previous experience in the operation of plant and machinery within a mining or heavy industry environment
Previous Supervisory experience within an open cut mining environment
Intermediate computer skills with proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, SAP and similar systems and databases
S1,2,3 Supervisor and G2 Qualifications are essential
Possess and maintain a current drivers licence
Benefits
Yancoal provides a range of benefits to appeal to the diverse needs of our employees. The following benefits are available to eligible employees:
Salary continuance and life insurance
20 weeks paid primary carers parental leave with no waiting period
Subsidised private health insurance
Fitness Passport - discounted workplace health and fitness program
Study assistance
Flexible work arrangements
Employee Assistance Program
Access to financial education and banking discounts
Salary packaging including novated leasing and remote area benefits
Service recognition rewards
Discounts program including major department stores, cinemas, restaurants
Yancoal Australia values diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from people with diverse backgrounds including First Nations people, people with disability, people from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, and people that identify as LGBTQIA+.
Yancoal Australia is a Work180 Endorsed Employer and is committed to ensuring all employees can thrive in an inclusive workplace.
If this sounds like the opportunity you've been looking for, apply today and take the first step toward an exciting new journey with us!
Be careful of scams
Yancoal will never ask you to make any payment or to provide credit card details when applying for any of our positions. If you have any concerns regarding the authenticity of communications received representing Yancoal, please email recruitment@yancoal.com.au with the subject “Fraud Query”, outlining your concern.

Due to expansion and growth at Dawson, we are on the look-out for multiple Production Supervisors to join the team!
Dawson Mine
Steelmaking Coal is an essential ingredient in blast-furnace steel manufacture, producing steel used for critical building and infrastructure around the world.
Dawson Mine, located near Moura, is only a 2-hour drive from Rockhampton & Gladstone. It is a large and dynamic open cut steelmaking coal mine operation made up of two operating pits - Central and South. It commenced operations in 1961 and is one of the oldest coal mining operations in Central Queensland with no signs of slowing down. Dawson Mine is rich in history, it was the first mine in Australia to export coal to Japan in 1961 and was the first mine to introduce draglines into its operation in 1963 – join the legacy today!
Job Description
We are currently seeking an experienced Production Supervisor with a passion for Truck and Shovel Operations whilst maintaining safe production at Dawson Mine.
Reporting through to the Mining Superintendent, you will demonstrate a drive for safety as well as strong interpersonal and leadership skills. As an experienced supervisor, you will be responsible for managing and leading a large team of operators to ensure operational production standards are met.
You will effectively plan, monitor and coordinate personnel, equipment and resources to achieve Dawson’s set targets and schedules. This role will be of interest to those who enjoy coaching and mentoring an established crew, interesting investigative challenges and effectively working with other departments on site whilst maintaining strong relationships.
Qualifications
Relevant skills, knowledge and experience required for this role include:
To be successful in the role, you will have supervisor experience running a large team within an open cut mining operation.
Demonstrated experience with Truck and Shovel operations and a working knowledge of relevant legislation.
Hold the Supervisor & Risk Facilitator competencies .
OCE ticket is desirable but not mandatory.
Trainer & Assessor certification is desirable.
Additional information
Due to expansion, multiple full-time permanent positions based at Dawson Mine, Moura QLD
Attractive 7 on, 7 off day/night roster
Competitive Salary Packaging + 12% Superannuation
Annual bonus incentive program
Residential, FIFO  & DIDO options
Subsidised accommodation and company funded camp options provided
Relocation Assistance available
Gym/Health incentives, On-site Physio, On-site EAP
Anglo American company benefits
What we Offer
When you join Anglo American, you can expect to enjoy a competitive salary and benefits package. But more than this, you’ll find yourself in an environment where the opportunities for learning and growth are second to none. From technical training to leadership programmes, we bring out the best in our people. There are plenty of opportunities to move onwards and upwards too. We’re a large, successful multinational company – and we’re still growing all the time.
Who we are
We aim to lead the industry by pursuing ever safer and more responsible ways of working, demonstrating integrity, and showing care and respect for people and the planet. That means we are constantly seeking new opportunities to mine and process our products sustainably, using less water, less energy and more precise extraction technologies.  As the custodians of coal and other precious natural resources – diamonds , copper, platinum and other precious metals, iron ore and nickel – our extraordinary teams work safely and collaboratively, with the utmost consideration for local communities, our customers and the world at large.

Background checks
Successful candidates will be required to complete background screening which may include a criminal check and validation of qualifications.

Inclusion and Diversity
At Anglo American, we embrace change and promote an inclusive and diverse environment where you can bring your whole self to work, every day. We are adopting new approaches to recruitment, talent development, mentoring, parental leave, and flexible working to further support diversity across our business.

How we are committed to your safety
Nothing is more important to us than ensuring you return home safely after a day’s work. To make that happen, we have the most rigorous safety standards in the industry. Not only that, we’re also continually investing in new technologies – from drones to data analytics – that are helping to make mining safer.
How to apply
To apply for this role, please complete our online application form. You will have the opportunity to upload your CV and other relevant documentation as part of this process.

14 April 2026

 you'll be home after each shift and for roles

Our Weipa Operations includes two bauxite mines, processing facilities,
ship loaders, an export wharf, two ports, power stations, a rail network

The Opportunity
We are seeking an experienced Mechanical Supervisor to support the delivery of surface and underground capital projects at South32s Cannington Mine in Queensland.
With underground expansion works progressing, this role will be instrumental in supervising mechanical installation and construction activities across a range of capital projects including:
Ventilation infrastructure and fresh air raise installations
Large underground fan installations and associated infrastructure
Pump stations and dewatering systems
Underground fixed plant installations
Surface mechanical infrastructure upgrades
Structural and mechanical installation works associated with mine expansion
This is a frontline leadership role within the capital delivery team, ensuring mechanical works are executed safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards.
Contract & Roster
Initial 12-month contract 
8 days on / 6 days off FIFO roster
Direct flights to site
Meals and accommodation provided
Key Responsibilities
Reporting to the Project / Construction Lead, you will:
Supervise mechanical construction and installation activities across underground and surface capital projects
Coordinate contractors and site crews to deliver works safely and in line with approved schedules
Ensure compliance with safety, environmental and statutory requirements
Oversee installation of mechanical equipment, piping systems, structural components and ventilation infrastructure
Monitor quality control, alignment, installation tolerances and commissioning readiness
Manage work orders and track progress using SAP and reporting systems
Participate in planning and coordination meetings with engineering, operations and project stakeholders
Identify and mitigate execution risks impacting schedule, cost or safety
Drive strong contractor performance and accountability on site
Promote a culture of safety leadership and continuous improvement
About You
You are a practical, safety-focused mechanical leader with strong underground mining capital experience.
You will demonstrate:
Minimum 5+ years experience within a mining mechanical environment
5+ years experience in a supervisory or leading hand role
Proven experience supervising underground capital projects
Experience delivering ventilation systems, pump stations, or fixed plant installations
Trade qualification in Mechanical Fitting / Boilermaking / Heavy Mechanical or equivalent
Strong understanding of mechanical installation standards and QA/QC processes
Experience working with contractors in a project delivery environment
Working knowledge of SAP and Microsoft Office systems
Ability to resolve day-to-day technical and site coordination challenges
Strong communication skills across multidisciplinary project teams
Experience with underground ventilation fans, raise infrastructure, dewatering systems, or structural/mechanical integration projects will be highly regarded.
Why This Role?
This is a key opportunity to contribute to a major underground expansion program within a Tier 1 mining environment. You will play a direct role in delivering critical mechanical infrastructure that underpins long-term production capability.
If you are an experienced Mechanical Supervisor seeking a technically challenging FIFO capital role within a well-structured operation, we would welcome your application.

About BHP
At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential.With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.
Come and be a part of this success.Click here to learn more.
About The Role
Goonyella Drill & Blast Team is welcoming a new Blast Supervisor who can help shape a safe, high-performing and supportive work environment.
As a Blast Supervisor, you'll play a key role in coordinating daily blast activities to support safe and efficient production outcomes across the Goonyella operation. You'll work closely with a skilled and supportive team, ensuring high-quality planning, execution and monitoring of all drill and blast activities on site.
In this role, you will:
· Lead and support the Blast crew to deliver safe, compliant and effective blast outcomes
· Oversee daily shot preparations, timing, QA/QC checks and blast initiation
· Collaborate closely with Drill & Blast Engineers, Shotfirers, and Production teams to achieve schedule requirements
· Contribute to a culture that prioritises safety, teamwork, continuous improvement and clear communication
· Support training, coaching and development of team members
· Champion inclusive and respectful ways of working across the blast team
This is an opportunity to step into a leadership role where your guidance will shape team culture and operational performance.
About You
You're someone who brings steadiness, strong communication and a people-centred approach to leadership. You enjoy problem-solving, planning ahead and working with others to achieve great results. You're confident in your technical skills and equally confident in your ability to support a team through change, challenge and growth.
To be successful, you will have:
· Shotfirer accreditation / Queensland Shotfirer Tickets
· Previous supervisory experience within a Drill & Blast or blasting operations team on a coal mine site
· Strong knowledge of drill & blast planning, field operations and safety processes
· The ability to build trusting relationships and support others to perform at their best
· A calm, inclusive and proactive approach to decision-making
· A commitment to maintaining a positive safety culture and high operational standards
If you're looking for a role where you can lead with empathy, influence positive outcomes and contribute to a high-performing blast team, we want to welcome you.
About Our Process
At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are several checks, which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants' suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.
Supporting a Diverse Workforce
The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.
At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that's more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.
BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at inclusion@bhp.com.

We have an exciting opportunity for a Team Leader to take a key role in
leading the Dry mix Bagging Plant team at our Bulwer Island site. In
this hands-on leadership role, you will oversee day-to-day operations of
the dry mix bagging plant, ensuring safe, efficient, and high-quality
production. You will provide guidance and direction to your team,
support maintenance planning and reporting, and drive continuous
improvement across safety, people, and plant performance. This is your
chance to make a real impact on operations and team culture within a
dynamic manufacturing environment.

Your Opportunity

In this role you will:

-   Ensure Bulwer Island Packaged Product operations comply with Cement
    Australia's SHEQ policies, including developing and reviewing risk
    assessments, work instructions, and implementing corrective actions.

-   Lead operational activities to achieve safety, quality, maintenance,
    and production targets, investigating and troubleshooting issues as
    required.

-   Operate the dry mix bagging plant, forklift, and skid
    steer/front-end loader safely and efficiently to maximise throughput
    and quality.

-   Manage receivables, dispatch products, and support bulk plant
    operations as needed.

-   Conduct reactive and preventative maintenance, assist with
    shutdowns, and support maintenance planning.

-   Supervise and coordinate contractors, including issuing work permits
    and reviewing risk assessments.

-   Utilise SAP for maintenance notifications, work orders, production
    reporting, spare parts management, and purchase requisitions.

-   Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities across
    safety, people, plant, and process areas.

What You'll Bring

-   Trade qualification preferred.

-   Minimum 5+ years' experience in bagging plant operations, including
    maintenance of fixed plant equipment; team leadership and
    shutdown/maintenance planning experience - preferred.

-   Forklift, Skid Steer, Confined Space & Rescue, Working at Heights;
    Front End Loader ticket - preferred.

-   Strong leadership and team motivation skills with a proven ability
    to lead safely and effectively.

-   Solid knowledge of manufacturing processes, machine maintenance, and
    fault finding.

-   Experience with Programmable Control Systems and interpreting
    operational trends.

-   Familiarity with hand and power tools.

-   Basic computer literacy, including Microsoft Office, email and SAP.

-   A positive attitude towards continuous improvement and operational
    upgrades.

Who You Are

-   Safety First Mindset: Champions safety and compliance, identifies
    and mitigates risks, and isn\'t afraid to challenge unsafe actions.
    Leads by example with courage and integrity.

-   Delivery and Prioritisation Strength: Manages production demands and
    logistics effectively, communicates clearly, uses ERP systems
    confidently, acts with urgency and initiative, resilient and agile
    in fast-paced environments.

-   People's Leader: Inspiring and influential beyond organisational
    authority, empathetic and courageous, nurtures engagement and
    positivity within the team.

-   Analytical & Systems Thinker: data driven, identifies root causes,
    understands systemic relationships, and drives productivity through
    analytical thinking.

-   Mechanical and Technical Aptitude: Understands condition-based
    maintenance, collaborates effectively with maintenance teams,
    embraces CI frameworks.

-   Business Savy: Applies cost-benefit thinking and a business outcome
    mindset to all decisions.

Why Join Cement Australia?

-   Be Yourself: Be your authentic self in a diverse and dedicated
    workplace where we focus on achieving great results together.

-   Paid Parental Leave: We offer 12 weeks paid parental leave  to support your
    family life, ensuring you can cherish special moments with your
    loved ones.

-   Novated Leasing: Drive your dream car for less through our
    partnership with sgfleet, offering fantastic discounts on
    vehicles.

-   Discounted Products: Renovating? DIY'er? Save on your home
    improvement projects with exclusive discounts on concrete and quarry
    products.

-   Health & Well-being: Prioritise your health with discounted
    health insurance , and access to our EAP program

Who We Are

Cement Australia has been at the heart of the nation's infrastructure
for decades, proudly standing as Australia's leading supplier of
cementitious products and services.

With our operations now extending across all states and territories -
including expanded manufacturing and distribution capabilities in
Western Australia - our national footprint continues to strengthen to
support Australia's growing construction and infrastructure needs.

But we're more than just cement. Our diverse operations include heavy
industrial manufacturing, coal and limestone mining, bulk transport
distribution, rail and shipping logistics, and innovative waste
management through Geocycle, where we turn challenges into sustainable
solutions.

At Cement Australia, we believe in empowering our people with the tools,
support, and opportunities to make a real impact. Join a collaborative
team that values work-life balance and thrives on innovation. Together,
we're building a safer, healthier, and more sustainable future---driven
by a shared commitment to excellence and our core values.

What Else You Need to Know
